questions.text: Which of the following are considered the 'lifelines' of a national economy?
questions.explanation: Transport and communication are called lifelines because they facilitate the movement of goods, services, and information, integrating the economy.

questions.text: The Golden Quadrilateral Super Highways project is implemented by which organization?
questions.explanation: The National Highways Authority of India (NHAI) is responsible for the implementation and maintenance of the Golden Quadrilateral project.

questions.text: Which cities are connected by the Golden Quadrilateral?
questions.explanation: The Golden Quadrilateral connects the four major metro cities of India: Delhi, Mumbai, Chennai, and Kolkata.

questions.text: The North-South Corridor connects which two extreme points of India?
questions.explanation: The North-South corridor links Srinagar in Jammu & Kashmir with Kanyakumari in Tamil Nadu.

questions.text: The East-West Corridor connects which two cities?
questions.explanation: The East-West corridor connects Silchar in Assam and Porbandar in Gujarat.

questions.text: National Highways are primary road systems maintained by which authority?
questions.explanation: The Central Public Works Department (CPWD) is responsible for the construction and maintenance of National Highways.

questions.text: Which historic road is known as Sher Shah Suri Marg?
questions.explanation: National Highway No. 1, connecting Delhi and Amritsar, is historically known as Sher Shah Suri Marg.

questions.text: The organization responsible for constructing and maintaining roads in the bordering areas of the country is:
questions.explanation: BRO was established in 1960 for the development of roads of strategic importance in the northern and north-eastern border areas.

questions.text: Roads built under the Pradhan Mantri Grameen Sadak Yojana connect:
questions.explanation: This scheme provides special impetus to link every village in the country to a major town by an all-weather motorable road.

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT an advantage of road transport over rail transport?
options: Economical for long distances with heavy goods
Feeder to other modes of transport
questions.explanation: Railways are more economical and efficient for transporting heavy goods over very long distances compared to roads.

questions.text: The first train in India steamed off in 1853 between which two stations?
questions.explanation: The first railway line in India was opened between Mumbai and Thane, covering a distance of 34 km.

questions.text: Which geographical factor significantly influenced the dense rail network in the Northern Plains?
options: Vast level land and high population density
questions.explanation: The level land, high population, and rich agricultural resources provided the most favorable conditions for railway growth in the North.

questions.text: The development of the Konkan railway has facilitated the movement of passengers and goods along the:
questions.explanation: The Konkan railway runs along the western coast of India, connecting major ports and cities through a difficult terrain.

questions.text: Which mode of transport is the most convenient for carrying liquids and gases over long distances?
questions.explanation: Pipelines reduce trans-shipment losses and delays, making them ideal for oil, gas, and slurry.

questions.text: Which is the cheapest mode of transport for bulky and heavy goods?
questions.explanation: Waterways are the most fuel-efficient and environment-friendly mode of transport for heavy cargo.

questions.text: The National Waterway No. 1 is between:
questions.explanation: NW1 is on the Ganga river between Allahabad and Haldia (1620 km).

questions.text: Which is the premier iron ore exporting port of India?
questions.explanation: Marmagao port in Goa accounts for about fifty percent of India's iron ore export.

questions.text: Which port was the first developed soon after independence to ease the volume of trade on Mumbai port?
questions.explanation: Kandla (now Deendayal Port) in Kuchchh was the first port developed after independence following the loss of Karachi port to Pakistan.

questions.text: Which is the deepest landlocked and well-protected port on the east coast?
questions.explanation: Vishakhapatnam is a landlocked port, originally conceived as an outlet for iron ore exports.

questions.text: Which of the following is an inland riverine port?
questions.explanation: Kolkata is a riverine port situated on the Hugli river, serving a very large and rich hinterland.

questions.text: Air transport was nationalized in the year:
questions.explanation: The Indian government nationalized air transport in 1953 to provide better connectivity across the country.

questions.text: Pawan Hans Helicopters Ltd. provides services to:
questions.explanation: Pawan Hans provides helicopter services to the Oil and Natural Gas Corporation and to inaccessible areas like the North East.

questions.text: Which of the following belongs to the 'First Class Mail' category in Indian postal network?
questions.explanation: Cards and envelopes are airlifted between stations and considered first-class mail.

questions.text: To facilitate quick delivery of mails in large towns and cities, how many mail channels have been introduced?
questions.explanation: The six channels are Rajdhani Channel, Metro Channel, Green Channel, Business Channel, Bulk Mail Channel, and Periodical Channel.

questions.text: The difference between the total value of exports and imports of a country is called its:
questions.explanation: Balance of trade is favorable when exports exceed imports and unfavorable when imports exceed exports.

questions.text: When the value of exports exceeds the value of imports, it is called:
questions.explanation: A favorable balance indicates that the country is earning more from foreign trade than it is spending.

questions.text: India has the largest ________ network in the world.
questions.explanation: The Indian postal network is the largest in the world, handling both personal and business communication.

questions.text: Digital India is an umbrella programme to prepare India for a:
questions.explanation: Digital India aims to transform the entire ecosystem of public services through the use of information technology.

questions.text: Which of the following is a means of mass communication?
questions.explanation: Mass communication provides entertainment and creates awareness among people about various national programmes and policies.

questions.text: Which of the following is the oldest artificial port of India?
questions.explanation: Chennai is one of the oldest artificial ports, ranked next to Mumbai in terms of volume of trade and cargo.

questions.text: What is the importance of tourism as a trade in India?
options: It provides support to local handicrafts
It helps in development of international understanding
questions.explanation: Tourism is a significant industry in India, contributing to economic growth and cultural exchange.

questions.text: The length of road per 100 sq. km of area is known as:
questions.explanation: Road density varies across India, from very low in Jammu & Kashmir to very high in Kerala.

questions.text: In the tribal areas of North-East India, which mode of transport is most preferred?
questions.explanation: Due to dissected relief and dense forests, air travel (helicopters) is the most efficient way to travel in these areas.

questions.text: The major objective of the Super Highways is to reduce ______ between the mega cities of India.
questions.explanation: Super Highways are designed for high-speed travel to save time and reduce travel distance.

questions.text: Which of the following is a major challenge for Indian Railways?
options: Thefts and damaging of railway property
questions.explanation: Despite being the main artery of transport, railways face several human-induced problems.

questions.text: How many major ports does India have along its long coastline?
questions.explanation: India has 12 major and 200 notified non-majors (minor/intermediate) ports.

questions.text: Which of the following ports is an outlet for the export of iron ore concentrates from Kudremukh mines?
questions.explanation: New Mangalore port in Karnataka caters to the export of iron ore from the Kudremukh mines.

questions.text: STD stands for:
questions.explanation: India has one of the largest telecom networks in Asia, with widespread STD facility.

questions.text: Which of the following is considered a 'Second Class Mail' in India?
questions.explanation: Second-class mail is carried by surface mail, covering land and water transport.

questions.text: The primary goal of developing the North-South and East-West corridors is to:
options: Facilitate movement between extremes of the country
questions.explanation: These corridors are part of the Super Highway project to integrate the entire country geographically.
